sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Emily, presented with sudden visual distortions and blurring of her vision.
She reports difficulty adjusting to dim lighting, specifically when dining at restaurants.
She denies any other symptoms.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of macular degeneration.
She is a smoker, consuming one pack of cigarettes per day, and has been trying to quit but has not been successful.
She reports taking her blood pressure medication sporadically, but does not keep a regular log of her readings.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms outside of her visual disturbances.
Current medications: I prescribed blood pressure medication, but the specific medication and dosing information was not provided.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The anterior segment examination revealed posterior chamber intraocular lenses in both eyes, clear corneas, and no MVI.
The cup-to-disc ratio is 0.2 in the right eye and 0.3 in the left eye.
The fundus examination of the right eye showed the retina to be attached, with no tears or holes.
The posterior segment exam revealed drusen in the macula, thickening of the retina, subretinal fluid, and exudates accumulation, and positive for hemorrhage in the left eye.

Test Results: The diagnostic tests, including OCT and fluorescein angiography, revealed drusen deposit, subretinal fluid accumulation, choroidal neovascular membrane, and focal areas of leakage in the right eye consistent with neovascular age-related macular degeneration.
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient has neovascular age-related macular degeneration, a condition that typically leads to severe vision loss due to the growth of abnormal blood vessels beneath the retina, causing fluid and blood leakage.
Problem list: Neovascular age-related macular degeneration; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Lucentis, an injection to be administered once a month into the vitreous portion of the eye, to slow the rate of vision decline or stop further vision loss.
Injections will be given regularly over a period of time.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up appointments as needed to monitor the progression of the disease and adjust treatment as necessary.
